title: Crossing Target Engagement
summary: Techniques for engaging targets moving perpendicular to line of fire.
details: Crossing targets are the most difficult to hit - maximum angular velocity means maximum lead requirement. Fire ahead of the target's path, not at the target. Use predictive bursts: place fire where target will cross. Against fighters, use spread patterns to increase hit probability. Alternatively, request pilot adjust to pursuit or head-on approach which reduces crossing angle.
annotations: Crossing shots are low-probability - accept lower accuracy.
Pursuit geometry is preferable when possible.
